title: Kernel orthestrationについて、codexだけでなくglmやclaudeとの協奏として設定されているか確認せよ
summary_text: - **Likely failure mode (current):** smoke runs launcher under pipe-captured stdio (`subprocess.run(..., capture_output=True)` pattern), so Codex detects non-TTY and fails with `stdin is not a terminal`. This is a harness artifact, not necessarily a production failure. [kernel smoke harness](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L122) [launcher uses guard->codex interactive path](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L313) - **Minimal robust fix:** run launcher smoke inside a **PTY** and keep marker-based startup validation. 1. Replace direct `subprocess.run(...capture_output...)` smoke call with Python `pty.openpty()` + `Popen(..., stdin=slave, stdout=slave, stderr=slave, start_new_session=True)`. 2. Stream PTY output until timeout and pass only when all are seen: - `preflight: PASS` - `Kernel orchestration is active ...` - `Bootstrap target: 6+ lanes (minimum 6).` - `Lane manifest:` with `>=6` lane lines. 3. On pass, terminate process group (startup smoke is complete); on timeout/error, fail closed. 4. Keep strict env defaults (`CODEX_KERNEL_REQUIRE_GUARD=1`, `CODEX_KERNEL_PROVIDER_CHECK=1`, bootstrap enabled). - **Why this is highest leverage:** it preserves the **supported production path** while removing TTY false negatives, unlike falling back to bare `/kernel` or non-launcher execution. [supported path contract](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/CODEX.md#L51) [bare slash non-SLO](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/CODEX.md#L58) - **Tradeoffs managed:** - Avoids false negatives from non-TTY harness. - Avoids false positives from upstream slash behavior drift. - Still not a full end-to-end completion test (it is startup-only by design), but now validates real guarded startup semantics.
